text:
- Over several weeks, Mo collects together all the information she needs to fill in Form E fully.

- Mo also starts collecting the documents she must attach to Form E as evidence of what she says in the form.

- Mo fills in her Form E. (Mo uses the Advicenow film How to fill in your financial statement to help her.)

- Mo and Pat agree a date (in good time before the deadline set by the court) when they will send their Form E to the court and a copy to each other.

- Mo sends her Form E to the court and a copy to Pat as agreed. Pat does the same with his Form E.

- Mo tries to agree with Pat on the contents of the Case summary (ES1) and the Schedule of assets and income (ES2) and the valuation of their home.

- Mo reads through Pat’s Form E carefully. She makes a list of questions about things that are not clear and about an insurance policy that he does not mention.

- Mo prepares

- ES1 case summary
- ES2 schedule of assets
- The agreed estate agent valuation of the family home
- Three sets of information about possible properties that would meet her housing needs and another three sets of information about possible properties that would meet Pat’s needs
- Form H
- Information on her borrowing capacity from a mortgage adviser (who Mo and Pat jointly ask to provide, if possible)
- A questionnaire
- A position statement
- Form G

- Mo sends them to the court and copies to Pat in time to meet the deadline.

- Mo and Pat both fill in Form FM5 and send a copy to each other and the court before the first hearing.

- Mo and Pat go to court for the first appointment. They both take copies of their documents with them and a notebook and pen.

- At the first appointment , the judge encourages Mo and Pat to try mediation again. The judge also tells them about all the things each of them will need to provide to give them the best chance of coming to an agreement at next hearing. The judge fixes a date for the Financial Dispute Resolution appointment. This is a meeting where the judge tries to resolve your financial dispute with your help and agreement - for more information about this hearing see The financial dispute resolution (FDR) appointment .
